title: Cardiovascular screening diagnostic and monitoring systems and methods
abstract: A system and method for providing an indication of at least LVD (Left Ventricular Dysfunction), the system including at least one temperature sensor, at least one body activity sensor, a time/temperature ascertainer operative to receive inputs from the at least one temperature sensor and from the at least one body activity sensor and to provide output indications of the skin temperature before the body activity, during the body activity and after the body activity, and a correlator operative to correlate the output indications with accumulated clinical data to existence of at least LVD, the correlator providing at least an output indication of at least one of presence of LVD and absence of LVD. A monitoring system and method for providing an indication of a Left Ventricular Dysfunction (LVD) - related condition that if not treated could require hospitalization (LVD -RCTIFNTCRH) in an LVD person.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD (Left Ventricular Dysfunction), the system comprising: at least one temperature sensor providing an output indication based on skin temperature at at least one location on a person at a plurality of given times; at least one body activity sensor providing an output indication of at least termination of body activity; a time/temperature ascertainer operative to receive inputs from said at least one temperature sensor and from said at least one body activity sensor and to provide skin temperature change output indications based on said skin temperature at at least one of before said body activity, during said body activity and after said body activity; and a correlator operative to correlate said skin temperature change output indications with accumulated clinical data relating changes in skin temperature at at least one of before body activity, during body activity and after body activity to existence of at least LVD, said correlator providing at least an output indication of at least one of presence of LVD and absence of LVD. 2. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD (Left Ventricular Dysfunction) according to claim 1 and wherein said at least one body activity sensor also provides an output indication indicating at least onset of body activity and an output related to an extent of body activity. 3. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 1 or claim 2 and wherein said at least one temperature sensor and said at least one body activity sensor respectively measure temperature and body activity at two distinct regions of a person's body. 4. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 1 or claim 2 and wherein said at least one temperature sensor and said at least one body 

 activity sensor respectively measure temperature and body activity at a single region of a person's body. 5. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 1 or claim 2 and wherein said at least one temperature sensor and said at least one body activity sensor respectively measure temperature and body activity such that said temperature represents skin temperature at a body region which is less active than a region which is principally undergoing body activity. 6. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of the preceding claims and wherein said at least one body activity sensor is embodied in a treadmill. 7. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of the preceding claims and wherein said temperature sensor measures skin temperature on a person's wrist. 8. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of the preceding claims and wherein said body activity sensor is mounted on a portion of the person's body which is undergoing physical activity while said temperature sensor is mounted on a portion of the person's body other than that portion undergoing physical activity. 9. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of the preceding claims and wherein: physical activity of the person is measured from a starting point in time designated time B at which the person is in a standing position after resting while sitting down; the onset of physical exertion begins at a point in time designated C; and said physical exertion is terminated at a point in time designated D. 10. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 9 wherein: a time separation between point in times B and C is approximately 5 minutes; a time separation between point in times C and D is approximately 5 minutes; and a further measuring point in time, designated point in time E, is established at approximately 5 minutes following point in time D. 11. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 10 wherein an MDST dynamic for time periods B - C, C - D and D - E is equivalent to dynamics shown in one of lines 7, 8, 9, and 10 in the Dynamics Table of Fig. 1 for a non-LVD person. 12. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 10 wherein an MDST dynamic for time periods B - C, C - D and D - E is equivalent to dynamics shown in one of lines 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 in the Dynamics Table of Fig. 1 for an LVD person. 13. A system according to any of claims 10 - 12 and wherein said body activity sensor provides outputs indicating ONSET OF PHYSICAL ACTIVITY (OOPA), TERMINATION OF PHYSICAL ACTIVITY (TOPA) and DISTANCE TRAVELLED DURING PHYSICAL ACTIVITY (DTD A). 14. A system according to claim 13 and also comprising a minimum exertion level calculator receiving said outputs of said body activity sensor and providing an output indicating whether a minimum threshold for physical exertion has been exceeded between the OOPA and the TOPA. 15. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of the preceding claims and also comprising at least one of at least one temperature sensor 

 to measure ambient temperature during diagnosis and at least one humidity sensor to measure ambient humidity during diagnosis. 16. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 15 and wherein said at least one of at least one temperature sensor and at least one humidity sensor is operative to correct ambient effects on body during diagnosis. 17. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 11 or claim 12 and wherein said MDST dynamic is provided as an output from said time/temperature ascertainer to said correlator. 18. A system for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 11 or claim 12 and wherein said MDST dynamic is based on differences between the measured temperature during said time periods B - C, C - D and D - E. 19. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD (Left Ventricular Dysfunction), the method comprising: sensing a skin temperature of a subject at at least one location on a person at a plurality of given times; providing a plurality of skin temperature output indications based on said sensing; sensing body activity of said subject and providing an output indication of at least onset and termination of said body activity; ascertaining skin temperature change output indications of said subject at at least one of before said onset of said body activity, during said body activity and after termination of body activity based on said plurality of skin temperature output indications and said output indication of onset and termination of said body activity; correlating said skin temperature change output indications of said subject with accumulated clinical data relating changes in skin temperature at at least one of before body activity, during body activity and after body activity to existence of at least LVD; and 

 providing at least an output indication of at least one of presence of LVD and absence of LVD. 20. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 19 and wherein said sensing a skin temperature and said sensing body activity respectively comprise sensing skin temperature and sensing body activity at two distinct regions of a person's body. 21. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 19 and wherein said sensing a skin temperature and said sensing body activity respectively comprise sensing skin temperature and sensing body activity at a single region of a person's body. 22. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 19 and wherein said sensing a skin temperature and said sensing body activity respectively comprise sensing skin temperature at a body region which is less active than a region which is principally undergoing body activity. 23. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of claims 19 - 22 and wherein: physical activity of the person is measured from a starting point in time designated time B at which the person stands up after resting while sitting down; the onset of physical exertion begins at a point in time designated C; said physical exertion is terminated at a point in time designated D. 24. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 23 wherein: a time separation between point in times B and C is approximately 5 minutes; a time separation between point in times C and D is approximately 5 minutes; and 

 a further measuring point in time, designated point in time E, is established at approximately 5 minutes following point in time D. 25. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 24 wherein an MDST dynamic for time periods B - C, C - D and D - E is equivalent to dynamics shown in one of lines 7, 8, 9, and 10 in the Dynamics Table of Fig. 1 for a non-LVD person. 26. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to claim 24 wherein an MDST dynamic for time periods B - C, C - D and D - E is equivalent to dynamics shown in one of lines 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 in the Dynamics Table of Fig. 1 for an LVD person. 27. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of claims 19 - 26 and also comprising sensing ambient temperature in order to correct ambient effects on body during diagnosis. 28. A method for providing an indication of at least LVD according to any of claims 19 - 27 and also comprising sensing ambient humidity in order to correct ambient effects on body during diagnosis. 29. A monitoring system for providing an indication of a Left Ventricular Dysfunction (LVD)-related condition that if not treated could require hospitalization (LVD-RCTIFNTCRH) in an LVD person, the system comprising: at least one temperature sensor providing an output indication based on skin temperature at at least one location on a person at a plurality of given times; at least one body activity sensor providing an output indication of at least termination of body activity; a time/temperature ascertainer operative to receive inputs from said at least one temperature sensor and from said at least one body activity sensor to provide output indications of said skin temperature at at least one of onset, during, and termination of body activity and thereafter; and 

 a monitoring comparator operative to receive said output indications from said time/temperature ascertainer representing at least two monitoring sessions which are mutually separated in time and to compare said output indications, said monitoring comparator providing at least an output indication of an LVD- RCTIFNTCRH. 30. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD -RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 29 and wherein said monitoring comparator also receives an input from said at least one body activity sensor. 31. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 29 and wherein said monitoring comparator also receives a personal data input. 32. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 29 and wherein said monitoring comparator also receives an input comprising stored results from one or more previous monitoring sessions of at least one LVD person other than the LVD person being monitored. 33. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 32 and wherein said monitoring comparator is operative to compare said output indications from said time/temperature ascertainer to said stored results from said one or more previous monitoring session of said at least one LVD person other than the LVD person being monitored. 34. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 29 and wherein said at least one temperature sensor and said at least one body activity sensor respectively measure temperature and body activity at a single region of a person's body. 35. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 29 and wherein said at least one temperature 

 sensor and said at least one body activity sensor respectively measure temperature and body activity at two different regions of a person's body. 36. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 35 and wherein said at least one temperature sensor measures skin temperature at a body region which is less active than a region which is principally undergoing body activity. 37. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 35 or claim 36 and wherein said body activity sensor is mounted on a portion of the person's body which is undergoing physical exertion and said temperature sensor is mounted on a portion of the person's body other than that portion undergoing physical exertion. 38. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 34 - 37 and wherein said temperature sensor measures skin temperature on a person's wrist. 39. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of the preceding claims 29 - 38 and wherein said at least one body activity sensor is embodied in a treadmill. 40. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of the preceding claims 29 - 39 and wherein: physical exertion of the person is measured from a starting point in time designated time A at which the person is standing and at rest; the onset of physical exertion begins at a point in time designated B; and said physical exertion is terminated at a point in time designated C. 41. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 40 wherein: a time separation between points A and B is approximately 5 minutes; 

 a time separation between points in time B and C is approximately 5 minutes; and a further measuring point in time, designated point in time D, is established at approximately 5 minutes following point in time C. 42. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 41 wherein measured differential skin temperature (MDST) relative to point in time C (MDST(C)) at at least one of points in time B and D is different from previous MDST(C) at said at least one point in time B and D following a change in LVD severity and/or progression. 43. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 41 wherein MDST(C) at points in time B and D is similar to previous MDST(C) at said points in time B and D following a lack of change in LVD severity and/or progression. 44. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 29 - 43 and wherein said at least one body activity sensor provides outputs indicating ONSET OF PHYSICAL EXERTION (OOPE), TERMINATION OF PHYSICAL EXERTION (TOPE) and DISTANCE TRAVELLED DURING PHYSICAL EXERTION (DTDE). 45. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 44 and also comprising a minimum exertion level calculator receiving said outputs of said at least one body activity sensor and providing an output indicating whether a minimum threshold for physical exertion has been exceeded between the OOPE and the TOPE. 46. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 29 - 39 and wherein: said body activity of the person is measured from a starting point in time designated time E at which the person is sitting and at rest; 

 said person begins to stand up at a point in time designated time Fl; and said person completes standing up at a point in time designated time F2. 47. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 46 wherein: a time separation between points E and Fl is approximately 5 minutes; a time separation between points Fl and F2 is approximately 5 seconds; a time separation between point in time F2 and a reference point in time G is approximately 2 minutes; and at least one further measuring point in time, designated at least one of HI, H2 & H3, is established at approximately at least one of 1 minute, 2 minutes and 3 minutes following point in time G. 48. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 47 wherein: at least two of said points in time HI, H2 & H3, are established following point in time G. 49. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 48 wherein: all of said points in time HI, H2 & H3 are established following point in time G. 50. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 47 - 49 and wherein a measured differential skin temperature (MDST) relative to point in time G (MDST(G)) at at least one of points in time HI, H2 and H3 is different from a previous MDST(G), at said at least one of points in time HI, H2 and H3 following a change in LVD severity and/or progression. 51. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 47 - 49 and wherein similarity between 

 MDST(G) at points in time HI, H2 and H3 in an earlier test and MDST(G) at said points in time HI, H2 and H3 in a later test is indicative of a lack of change in LVD severity and/or progression between the earlier test and the later test. 52. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 47 - 51 and wherein said body activity sensor provides outputs indicating ONSET OF POSITION CHANGE (OOPC), TERMINATION OF POSITION CHANGE (TOPC) and POSITION CHANGE INERTIAL DATA (PCID). 53. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 52 and also comprising a body position change calculator receiving said outputs of said body activity sensor and providing an output indicating whether a qualifying position change has been performed between the OOPC and the TOPC as well as the TYPE OF POSITION CHANGE (TYPC). 54. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of the preceding claims 29 - 53 and also comprising at least one of at least one temperature sensor to measure ambient temperature during monitoring and at least one humidity sensor to measure ambient humidity during monitoring of said person. 55. A monitoring system for providing an indication of LVD-RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 54 and wherein said at least one of at least one temperature sensor and at least one humidity sensor measures ambient conditions in order to correct ambient effects on said person during said monitoring. 56. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person, the method comprising: sensing a skin temperature of a subject at at least one location on a person at a plurality of given times; 

 providing a plurality of skin temperature output indications based on said sensing; sensing body activity of said subject and providing an output indication of at least termination of said body activity; ascertaining skin temperature of said subject at at least one of onset of body activity, during body activity, termination of body activity and thereafter based on said plurality of skin temperature output indications and said output indication of at least termination of said body activity; correlating said skin temperature of said subject at said at least one of onset of body activity, during body activity, termination of body activity and thereafter with pre-established clinical skin temperature baseline data for the same specific individual relating changes in skin temperature indications at at least one of onset of body activity, during body activity and at termination of body activity and thereafter to severity and/or progression of at least LVD; and providing at least an output indication of at least a change in LVD severity and/or progression. 57. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 56 and wherein said sensing a skin temperature and said sensing body activity respectively comprise sensing skin temperature and sensing body activity at two distinct regions of a person's body. 58. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 56 and wherein said sensing a skin temperature and said sensing body activity respectively comprise sensing skin temperature and sensing body activity at a single region of a person's body. 59. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 56 and wherein said sensing a skin temperature and said sensing body activity respectively comprise sensing skin temperature at a body region which is less active than a region which is principally undergoing body activity. 60. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 56 - 59 and wherein: physical exertion of the person is measured from a starting point in time designated time A at which the person is standing and at rest; the onset of physical exertion begins at a point in time designated B; said physical exertion is terminated at a point in time designated C; a time separation between points A and B is approximately 5 minutes; a time separation between points in time B and C is approximately 5 minutes; and a further measuring point in time, designated point in time D, is established at approximately 5 minutes following point in time C. 61. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 60 wherein said output indication of at least a change in LVD severity and/or progression indicates the absence of a change in LVD severity and/or progression when measured differential skin temperature relative to point in time C is similar to measured differential skin temperature relative to point in time C in previous measurements. 62. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 60 wherein said output indication of at least a change in LVD severity and/or progression indicates the presence of a change in LVD severity and/or progression when measured differential skin temperature relative to point in time differs from measured differential skin temperature relative to point in time C in previous measurements. 63. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 56 - 62 and wherein: said body activity of the person is measured from a starting point in time designated time E at which the person is sitting and at rest; said person begins to stand up at a point in time designated time Fl; 

 said person completes standing up at a point in time designated time F2; a time separation between points E and Fl is approximately 5 minutes; a time separation between points Fl and F2 is approximately 5 seconds; a time separation between point in time F2 and a later reference point in time G is approximately 2 minutes; and at least one further measuring point in time, designated at least one of HI, H2 & H3, is established at approximately at least one of 1 minute, 2 minutes and 3 minutes following point in time G. 64. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 63 wherein a measured differential skin temperature(MDST) relative to point in time G (MDST(G)), at at least one of points in time HI, H2 and H3 that is different from a previous MDST(G) at said at least one of points in time HI, H2 and H3 indicates a change in LVD severity and/or progression for an LVD person. 65. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 63 wherein similarity between MDST(G) at points in time HI, H2 and H3 in an earlier test and MDST(G) at said points in time HI, H2 and H3 in a later test is indicative of a lack of change in LVD severity and/or progression between the earlier test and the later test. 66. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 56 - 65 and also comprising sensing at least one of ambient temperature and ambient humidity during said sensing a skin temperature. 67. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 66 and also comprising correcting ambient effects on said person during said sensing a skin temperature. 68. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 56 - 67 wherein said correlating comprises receiving a personal data input. 69. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to any of claims 56 - 68 and wherein said correlating comprises receiving stored results from one or more monitoring sessions of at least one LVD person other than said subject. 70. A computerized method for providing an indication of LVD- RCTIFNTCRH in an LVD person according to claim 69 and wherein said correlating comprises comparing said skin temperature of said subject to said stored results from said one or more monitoring sessions of said at least one LVD person other than said subject.
